summary: Problem 10 is an algorithm-design and complexity-analysis task (preconditioned CG for RKHS-constrained CP decomposition on tensors with missing entries) rather than a deep theorem-proving problem, with a solvability score of 57%. The single tracked issue is a resolved seed/placeholder, and there is no substantive proof attempt on record to evaluate, leaving the best-proof status effectively empty despite '100 open issues' noise.
problems: No actual proof or algorithm derivation exists yet; the only issue was a placeholder seed task that was closed as 'no proof to evaluate.'

Mathematical blockers are unspecified because no derivation has been attempted — key gaps likely include rigorous convergence/conditioning bounds for the preconditioned CG iteration under the missing-entry (unaligned) sampling pattern.
The RKHS constraint interacts with the CP model in ways that need a precise operator formulation (Gram/kernel matrices into the normal equations) that has not been written down.
Complexity analysis of the Kronecker/Khatri-Rao matvec trick under missing entries is not yet established, so the claimed efficiency gains are unverified.
Reported metadata is inconsistent (best proof shows '100 open issues' while only 1 resolved placeholder issue is listed), making status assessment unreliable.
highlights: Task is well-scoped and tractable: all core ingredients (conjugate gradient, Kronecker/Khatri-Rao matvec tricks, RKHS regularization) are standard and available.
Authored by domain experts (Kolda, Ward), suggesting the intended construction is sound and follows known tensor-decomposition machinery.
57% solvability indicates a realistic path to a complete writeup with structured algorithmic and complexity work rather than novel deep theory.
The placeholder issue is already resolved, so there is no genuine outstanding error blocking progress — only a missing initial draft.

suggested_todos.title: Draft the formal RKHS-constrained CP optimization problem, including the masked/least-squares objective for missing entries and the kernel Gram operator in the normal equations
Specify the preconditioned CG algorithm and prove its per-iteration matvec cost using Khatri-Rao/Kronecker structure under the missing-entry sampling pattern
Establish convergence/conditioning bounds: characterize the spectrum of the preconditioned normal-equations operator and the preconditioner's effect
Complete the end-to-end complexity analysis (per-iteration and total) and compare against unpreconditioned and dense baselines
Reconcile inconsistent issue metadata (100 open vs 1 resolved) and create concrete sub-issues for each proof component above
Add a small numerical validation or sanity-check experiment to support the efficiency and convergence claims
